title: RTSP/SDP Parser Deep Reverse Engineering
summary: Comprehensive audit of all RTSP and SDP parser code regions for unsafe string function calls. Found 69 total unsafe calls (24 strcpy, 4 sprintf, 41 sscanf) with ZERO stack canaries in any RTSP/SDP function. Three critical overflow patterns identified, but all require post-auth access under default configuration.

critical_findings.title: SDP Serializer snprintf Accumulator Overflow
critical_findings.vulnerability: snprintf loop accumulates return values in r4. When r4 > 2050, the size parameter (2050-r4) wraps to ~4GB unsigned, causing subsequent snprintf calls to write unbounded past the buffer.
critical_findings.pre_auth: POST-AUTH under default config (Digest). Pre-auth if RtspAuthType=0 (None).
critical_findings.exploitation: Send RTSP ANNOUNCE with SDP body containing enough a= attributes that total serialized length exceeds 2050 bytes. The accumulator wraps, snprintf writes unbounded, overwrites saved LR on stack. NX disabled = shellcode on stack.

critical_findings.title: RtspUrlParser Stack Buffer Overflow via strcpy
critical_findings.vulnerability: On parse failure path, strcpy copies buffer1 (sp+0x10) into buffer2 (sp+0x210, 512 bytes). Buffer1 is populated by earlier parsing with a 512-byte limit at one stage, but the initial URL copy may be unbounded.
critical_findings.pre_auth: POST-AUTH. RtspUrlParser called inside DESCRIBE/SETUP handlers, which run after session_process auth check.
critical_findings.additional_strcpy: 0x5d4dc (sub-function 0x5d430) copies raw URL on failure, dest is caller-supplied buffer of unknown size

critical_findings.title: RtspClient strcpy+sprintf Double Overflow
critical_findings.vulnerability: strcpy at 0x48e674 followed by sprintf at 0x48e68c into same 128-byte stack buffer. Source is from RTSP session data.
critical_findings.pre_auth: POST-AUTH (RTSP client code, not server processing)

rtsp_auth_model.default: Digest (2) -- factory default for Dahua cameras
rtsp_auth_model.auth_check_location: Called BEFORE per-method handlers in on_request

verdict: All RTSP/SDP overflow vulnerabilities are POST-AUTH under default Digest configuration. The pre-auth URL pre-parser (0x31cfb4) uses bounded strncpy. The vulnerable RtspUrlParser (0x5d5bc) is only invoked inside per-method handlers after auth verification. The SDP serializer overflow (0x33e1b8) is the most severe but requires authenticated RTSP session to trigger SDP re-serialization.
